column1: The Six Lamps are meditative supports for recognizing rigpa (pristine awareness). They represent the interplay between the physical body (channels, eyes), perception (space, mirror), and the nature of mind (light, pure space).
(Dzogchen Thögal, Tibetan Book of the Dead)
The Four Lamps of Thögal are visionary supports for the realization of the rainbow body and the direct perception of the luminous nature of mind during advanced Dzogchen practice.
